[h2]Nota Geral | Overall Rating[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]GTA IV Г© um daqueles jogos que aparecem uma vez por geraГ§ГЈo e redefinem o que um mundo aberto pode ser. A Rockstar nГЈo estava interessada em entregar o maior mapa ou o maior nГәmero de atividades. Ela estava interessada em contar uma histГіria de verdade, dentro de um mundo que respira, reage e pune. Niko Bellic nГЈo Г© um protagonista genГ©rico de sandbox. Ele Г© um imigrante com trauma de guerra tentando sobreviver em uma cidade que devora quem chega acreditando no sonho americano. A fГӯsica de ragdoll, a direГ§ГЈo de veГӯculos com peso real e um enredo que faz cada missГЈo importar fazem desse jogo um marco que o gГӘnero ainda nГЈo superou.[/b]

[i]GTA IV is one of those games that comes around once a generation and redefines what an open world can be. Rockstar wasn't interested in delivering the biggest map or the most activities. It was interested in telling a real story, inside a world that breathes, reacts, and punishes. Niko Bellic is not a generic sandbox protagonist. He is a war-scarred immigrant trying to survive in a city that devours those who arrive believing in the American dream. The ragdoll physics, the vehicle handling with real weight, and a story that makes every mission matter make this game a landmark the genre has yet to surpass.[/i]

[h2]Jogabilidade | Gameplay[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]Os carros tГӘm peso. Cada modelo reage de forma completamente diferente ao asfalto molhado de Liberty City, e uma curva mal calculada em alta velocidade nГЈo termina num drift bonito. Termina num capotamento. O motor Euphoria torna a fГӯsica orgГўnica e imprevisГӯvel, mudando completamente como vocГӘ planeja uma fuga da polГӯcia. O ragdoll do Niko e dos NPCs Г© no mesmo nГӯvel. Quando o Niko Г© atingido por um carro em alta velocidade, o corpo reage com um realismo que choca na primeira vez e continua impressionando na centГ©sima. Os NPCs voam, tropeГ§am, batem contra grades. Nada parece animaГ§ГЈo engessada. O Гәnico ponto negativo Г© a ausГӘncia total de uma oficina de tunagem. VocГӘ dirige carros com uma fГӯsica extraordinГЎria mas nГЈo pode modificar nada neles. Para quem vinha do San Andreas, isso dГіi.[/b]

[i]The cars have weight. Every model reacts completely differently to Liberty City's wet asphalt, and a poorly calculated turn at high speed doesn't end in a clean drift. It ends in a rollover. The Euphoria engine makes the physics organic and unpredictable, completely changing how you plan a police escape. Niko's ragdoll and the NPCs' are on the same level. When Niko is hit by a car at high speed, the body reacts with a realism that shocks you the first time and keeps impressing the hundredth. NPCs fly, stumble, slam into railings. Nothing feels like canned animation. The only downside is the complete absence of a tuning shop. You drive cars with extraordinary physics but can't modify anything on them. For those coming from San Andreas, that stings.[/i]

[h2]DuraГ§ГЈo | Duration[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]GTA IV Г© generoso sem ser excessivo. A campanha mantГ©m qualidade narrativa do inГӯcio ao fim sem recorrer a missГөes de enchimento. As atividades secundГЎrias, como os programas de rГЎdio e as saГӯdas com os amigos, adicionam camadas ao mundo sem parecer conteГәdo obrigatГіrio para inflar o contador de horas. As DLCs The Lost and Damned e The Ballad of Gay Tony entregam histГіrias paralelas que se cruzam com a campanha principal de um jeito que poucos jogos conseguem fazer funcionar.[/b]

[i]GTA IV is generous without being excessive. The campaign maintains narrative quality from beginning to end without resorting to filler missions. The side activities, like the radio programs and outings with friends, add layers to the world without feeling like mandatory content to inflate the hour counter. The DLCs The Lost and Damned and The Ballad of Gay Tony deliver parallel stories that intersect with the main campaign in a way few games can pull off.[/i]

[h2]Enredo | Story[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]A histГіria de Niko Bellic nГЈo Г© uma fantasia de poder. Гү um conto de consequГӘncias. Cada decisГЈo Г© movida por desespero, lealdade mal colocada e uma busca por redenГ§ГЈo que o jogo nunca resolve de forma satisfatГіria. E Г© exatamente isso que faz a narrativa funcionar. O Roman Г© um dos personagens secundГЎrios mais bem escritos do gГӘnero, e a relaГ§ГЈo entre os dois primos carrega o jogo emocionalmente do comeГ§o ao fim. Os antagonistas sГЈo credГӯveis porque o jogo leva tempo para construГӯ-los antes de usГЎ-los. O final, independente da escolha que vocГӘ faz, deixa uma cicatriz. E isso Г© o sinal de que uma histГіria foi contada de verdade.[/b]

[i]Niko Bellic's story is not a power fantasy. It is a tale of consequences. Every decision is driven by desperation, misplaced loyalty, and a search for redemption the game never resolves satisfyingly. And that is exactly what makes the narrative work. Roman is one of the best-written supporting characters in the genre, and the relationship between the two cousins carries the game emotionally from beginning to end. The antagonists are credible because the game takes its time building them before using them. The ending, regardless of the choice you make, leaves a scar. And that is the sign that a story was truly told.[/i]

[h2]Visual[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]Liberty City Г© uma conquista de direГ§ГЈo de arte. A recriaГ§ГЈo de Nova York tem identidade visual prГіpria, com a sujeira dos bairros perifГ©ricos, a iluminaГ§ГЈo noturna de Algonquin e a diferenГ§a palpГЎvel entre cada ilha. O jogo carrega a sua idade em certas texturas, mas a atmosfera cinza e pesada ainda funciona como pretendida. A chuva escorrendo pelas fachadas, o nevoeiro sobre o rio, os reflexos no asfalto molhado constroem um ambiente sem precisar de resoluГ§ГЈo 4K para convencer.[/b]

[i]Liberty City is a triumph of art direction. The recreation of New York has its own visual identity, with the grime of the outer boroughs, the nighttime lighting of Algonquin, and the palpable difference between each island. The game carries its age in certain textures, but the gray and heavy atmosphere still works as intended. Rain on facades, fog over the river, reflections on wet asphalt build an environment without needing 4K resolution to convince.[/i]

[h2]Audio[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]O design de ГЎudio Г© preciso onde mais importa. O som dos carros muda conforme a velocidade, o impacto dos ragdolls tem peso fГӯsico, e os tiros tГӘm uma secura que combina com o tom realista do jogo. O grande destaque Г© o rГЎdio. As estaГ§Гөes de GTA IV sГЈo excepcionais, com curadoria que vai do eastern europeu do Vladivostok FM ao hip hop do The Beat 102.7, e cada uma constrГіi uma camada a mais da identidade de Liberty City. A dublagem do Niko, feita por Michael Hollick, entrega um personagem que convence nos momentos de violГӘncia e nos de vulnerabilidade. Гү um trabalho vocal que sustenta a narrativa inteira.[/b]

[i]The audio design is precise where it matters most. The sound of cars changes with speed, the ragdoll impacts carry physical weight, and gunshots have a dryness that fits the game's grounded tone. The standout is the radio. GTA IV's stations are exceptional, with a curation ranging from the Eastern European flavor of Vladivostok FM to the hip hop of The Beat 102.7, each one adding another layer to Liberty City's identity. Niko's voice acting, performed by Michael Hollick, delivers a character that convinces in moments of violence and vulnerability alike. It is vocal work that carries the entire narrative.[/i]

This is my first review. While in many respect this is still a great game, you do need to know that it is old and has a number of compatibility problems on PC. First: make sure you have the FusionFix patch installed (I believe it downloads on Steam, but it is critical.) I also strongly recommend searching the internet and downloading a good trainer. Second: make sure you reduce the FPS to 30. Any higher and the motorcycles (which have to be driven to finish the game) are simply undrivable. Third: there is a serious bug that prevents you from getting on a helo while it is in the air. This is something you have to do to complete the final mission in the game. Unfortunately, the solution all over the internet about this (reduce the game to run on only 1 CPU core) no longer works on recent computers--they are just too fast even on only 1 CPU. I was able to complete the final mission because I have a programmable keyboard and was able to write a macro to repeatedly press the spacebar at 0.03 sec intervals. If you can't write macros for your keyboard, I know of no way to complete the game on a modern PC. This is very unfortunate because the story and gameplay is otherwise fantastic. The graphics are not up to modern standards, but still very good (fun to just drive around Liberty City), and the music on the car radios is outstanding. If you have a solution to the helo problem, I highly recommend the game if you like action/adventure. If you don't, you won't be able to complete the game--and I wouldn't recommend getting it because getting to the last mission and then getting stuck will likely prove very frustrating.

Context to Niko's life: when Tito died(the president of Niko's country) certain members of government who were exiled due to being involved with Hit--- WW2 axis group, returned to Niko's country and started propaganda against Yugo as a country. They wanted to split it. Eventually, they hired bombers to attack both sides and blamed it on each other. This started the Yugo civil war, and the hate between Serbs and Hervats. Niko was in that war.
Long after the war is when this takes place... and it's a like a little time capsule of both the mentality a man like my father had(Niko's) and America in the mid-2000's. It's quite a trip in 2026, both nostalgia, but also from an outsiders point of view.
Some of the satire hits too close to home now with the current political climate, especially the PLR ads that are.... just depressing to listen to now. They sound like actual conservative stations and people. I'll stay out of politics, as it spoils my lunch as much as it does anyone's else's.
Other satire is great, and there are many things and characters that REALLY remind me of high school, the amount of homophobia and transphobia is just unreal compared to now where it's an accepted norm(at least in Canada, staying out of politics). The music is about the same, some songs I can't believe are THAT old, while others really show their age.
I absolutely loved Niko's journey, as I never got to play this when it came out. I was in a very low point in life, and heavily played MMOs to escape reality, other games just couldn't cut it.
I am NOT okay with Rockstar's launcher downloading a bunch of random services and crap on my PC, so I played the majority of this offline, and I understand everyone who pirated this game for that very reason. It's not okay for an out-of-steam company to demand their own software and launcher in order to play their games, and I will always downvote a game if there's no way around it. Luckily, with this one, there was.
This is worth the price if you're 18 or all the way to 98, either way it's such a time capsule that can be dissected, enjoyed, and serves as quite a reminder of "what not to become". Peace. Thanks for reading.

I first saw this game in an internet cafe when I was around 6 or 7 years old. An older guy was playing it, and the game literally mesmerized me with its visuals and engine. It made me say, "Can a game really be like this?" I asked him to launch this game for me too and played it for a short while. A few years later, after we bought the game for our own PC at home, I had the opportunity to play it. At that age, I didn't understand anything about the game's story. The only things I cared about were fighting with people, rolling them down stairs, pushing them off cliffs, driving cars, and making people hit me so I could watch them get arrested by the police. These things gave me incredible pleasure. When I replayed the game after growing up, I saw how realistic, dark, and close to our world its story is among the GTA games. I think it has the best story in the series. Even in this era, the game engine still feels very good. A production that everyone should play.

Nostalgia.

This game is a wonderful time capsule from 2008. That includes the political and economic climate at the time, the "brown grey and realistic" aesthetic, as well as the PC port quality (which is abysmal and requires third party fixes to run at modern standards). Rockstar still had something to prove when they were making this one. 

Whether it's better than GTA V or not is debatable, and people do, but I personally think it's the best game in the series. With fusionfix and the radio downgrader that restores cut songs from the soundtrack, this game provides an experience that no other game can. This was Rockstar's first ever attempt at making a game that nobody else was able to at a time when everybody and their mother were still trying to make a clone of the older GTA classics that had already taken the world by storm. Almost 20 years later, I don't think any other game dev has managed to even get close to matching its quality. Many have tried, but nobody paid attention to the little things more than 2008 Rockstar did.

It loses a lot of features from San Andreas, which many people (myself included) considered a major downgrade at the time, but it offers an unforgettable story, amazing fictional version of NYC, ridiculous but still entirely believable characters, and the best physics for combat and driving that still haven't been matched to this day except for RDR1. I put many, many more hours into GTA V and their other games, but IV goes for quality over quantity every step of the way.

Tons of people complain about the driving physics, but they're the reason I constantly return to this game. The sense of speed and the control you need to have border on racing sim territory compared to any other game of its type, and I spend most of my time just racing around at max speed and crashing into stuff. Older muscle cars are made of steel and don't damage easily (but you're flying out the windshield), the new pop can aluminum ones crumple, old cars lack ABS, 4WD, FWD and RWD cars all handle entirely differently, and there's no artificial cap on the max speed the cars can go like in GTA V. The attention to every detail of the driving was a game changer. Never gets old. 

The Ballad of Gay Tony is also arguably one of the best game expansions ever made.

10/10, would reinstall and play every year again.

Not sure what to say that's already been covered, but Rockstar definitely went full in with Grand Theft Auto IV. The dark gritty story, physics, the characters/cultures you meet and see along the way, the typical unfiltered humor and the soundrack that shaped some of my musical taste just to mention a few things. Of course, Niko Bellic is a well written protagonist too. The game felt more real in a way, it's a balance between old and new and captures a certain time of the 2000's. GTA 4 is the HBO Original of video games, I'm sure if you have seen series like The Sopranos and The Wire etc you know exactly what I'm talking about (which they did take some insperation from). The DLC's were alright as well even if they might not have had the same impact. Wish sometimes I could experience this masterpiece all over again like it's the first time. All GTA games are great in their own way and there's probably one that reconates with you in a more specific way, and that for me would be IV.

I first saw this game in an internet cafe when I was around 6 or 7 years old. An older guy was playing it, and the game literally mesmerized me with its visuals and engine. It made me say, "Can a game really be like this?" I asked him to launch this game for me too and played it for a short while. A few years later, after we bought the game for our own PC at home, I had the opportunity to play it. At that age, I didn't understand anything about the game's story. The only things I cared about were fighting with people, rolling them down stairs, pushing them off cliffs, driving cars, and making people hit me so I could watch them get arrested by the police. These things gave me incredible pleasure. When I replayed the game after growing up, I saw how realistic, dark, and close to our world its story is among the GTA games. I think it has the best story in the series. Even in this era, the game engine still feels very good. A production that everyone should play.

GTA 4 is one of my favorite entries in the GTA series. IвҖҷve played most of the GTA games, except for the Game Boy titles. However, the PC version is lacking in many ways. A lot of effects from the console version are missing, and some of the вҖңfeaturesвҖқ they added, like increased traffic, feel poorly thought out. Cars constantly crash into each other, causing traffic jams on bridges, which hurts the gameplay experience.

That being said, mods really save the PC version. Right now, I have FusionFix installed, which restores many effects from the console version while also adding new ones. Performance-wise, itвҖҷs well known that the PC port was never optimized properly. I donвҖҷt have a modern high-end PC, but my specs are still far above the recommended requirements from back then.

I also think the audio is worse in the PC port. Maybe itвҖҷs because of my sound chip, but the sound feels flat and overly high-pitched. The story, however, is excellent вҖ” itвҖҷs dark and takes itself seriously. The physics are still in a league of their own. I love how the cars handle around corners, or how the Euphoria engine creates cinematic moments when you jump out of a vehicle.

The crashes and Euphoria physics were spectacular at the time, and those were the main reasons why I wanted to buy the game so badly. Back in 2008, I didnвҖҷt have a good PC or console, and I was also too young. In the end, IвҖҷd definitely recommend the game вҖ” as long as you install FusionFix or play it on console.

On its own, this is maybe one of the worst PC ports on Steam. Somehow has more bugs than the original 2008 release and LESS features (the number of removed songs alone is insane).

With mods (I recommend following Gillian's Guide), this game is exceptional.

The car handling, as many have already repeated, is initially frustrating. However, while there is a learning curve, I found mastering the driving a blast and certainly more fulfilling than the more arcade-y driving in GTA V. 

Also an improvement from GTA V, this installment features what I consider a more lived-in world with a clearer tone. While the game is a product of its time and may often look washed out, it complements the dark, grimy tone and is juxtaposed well in heavily saturated areas (most of Algonquin).

The constant calls to go bowling may be a little annoying, but I actually found spending time with Niko's friends an effective and natural incentive for players to interact with the world via mini-games, races, and side missions.

However, the game could use a better wanted system. It feels too hard to get stars and too easy to lose them (unless you handicap yourself by running on-foot). There doesn't seem to be much criminal activity to do outside of missions, other than running over pedestrians. 

If, for some reason, you want to play vanilla, just go play the original on console. However, if you don't mind spending 10 minutes installing some mods, this game offers the definitive GTA IV experience.

This game is a wonderful time capsule from 2008. That includes the political and economic climate at the time, the "brown grey and realistic" aesthetic, as well as the PC port quality (which is abysmal and requires third party fixes to run at modern standards). Rockstar still had something to prove when they were making this one. 

Whether it's better than GTA V or not is debatable, and people do, but I personally think it's the best game in the series. With fusionfix and the radio downgrader that restores cut songs from the soundtrack, this game provides an experience that no other game can. This was Rockstar's first ever attempt at making a game that nobody else was able to at a time when everybody and their mother were still trying to make a clone of the older GTA classics that had already taken the world by storm. Almost 20 years later, I don't think any other game dev has managed to even get close to matching its quality. Many have tried, but nobody paid attention to the little things more than 2008 Rockstar did.

It loses a lot of features from San Andreas, which many people (myself included) considered a major downgrade at the time, but it offers an unforgettable story, amazing fictional version of NYC, ridiculous but still entirely believable characters, and the best physics for combat and driving that still haven't been matched to this day except for RDR1. I put many, many more hours into GTA V and their other games, but IV goes for quality over quantity every step of the way.

Tons of people complain about the driving physics, but they're the reason I constantly return to this game. The sense of speed and the control you need to have border on racing sim territory compared to any other game of its type, and I spend most of my time just racing around at max speed and crashing into stuff. Older muscle cars are made of steel and don't damage easily (but you're flying out the windshield), the new pop can aluminum ones crumple, old cars lack ABS, 4WD, FWD and RWD cars all handle entirely differently, and there's no artificial cap on the max speed the cars can go like in GTA V. The attention to every detail of the driving was a game changer. Never gets old. 

The Ballad of Gay Tony is also arguably one of the best game expansions ever made.

This is my summation of GTA4
Returning to liberty city after GTA 3 and GTA LCS in this game is kind of magical; everything is bigger, more 3d, well detailed, and just a lot more... vertical for some reason. It is really enjoyable to explore this map of GTA 4 whilst driving as it has plenty of great drivable roads.

It has vehicular physics, that I as a man who has played all of the need for speed games including shift and prostreet as well as the forza horizon titles from the 4th till the 6th; greatly approve of, because while not realistic, they give a very interesting and relevant feel to cars. They simulate turning radius's, weight transfer, suspension effects, acceleration and braking effects very well. Brake too hard and the wheels lockup, ensuring that you crash into the car or the wall ahead of you. 

The gunplay, fighting and the combat system for its time were phenomenal and even now for an open world title like gta are not really surpassed by much except for dying light perhaps; but that is a different genre. Mission and storyline are very realistic; characters are carefully written to ensure a consistent and understandable story-line; crime depiction is realistic and dark which is similar to what real life crime-worlds must be like. There is a lot of content to explore, stuff to do; unique interactions and so on.

The negatives of this game start from the graphics and performance department.
Graphically it is obviously a step up from the original gta trilogy but unlike how the graphics are supposed to be represented with volumetric lighting and good fog effects as seen on the console version; this game just looks flat most of the time. When it rains, then it replicates the og gta 4 graphic and mood magic; but otherwise it just looks flat and I would even say a bit jagged because of how roughly done the graphics are.

Game engine sucks because it was designed for vulkan based playstations and xbox's but most pcs do not run it using that and use the poor d3d9 translation which can also on vulkan supported gpus be swapped out with dxvk and the like to simulate vulkan running environments but no. I have seen that the performance is still kind of mid. Fusionfix is supposed to act as a good way of running this game reliably but it only worsens performance on my system; and of all things causes parking issues wherein saved cars do not load.

So, I play this game in its base state which aint ideal; but funny enough, besides the microstutters it runs a lot more stable without mods than with them. So if mods aint working; cap your fps to 40 using nvidia as well as entering a line "availablevidmem 8192" or whatever value your ram can support; and just run it as is. Of course, what I say for me may not work for you and dxvk might let you get 120 some fps; but that just points out how unpredictably this port reacts to different pc's and internal settings.

Now some of you will say, including me that this is a bad port which it is because of how much вҷҘвҷҘвҷҘвҷҘ it misses out on in terms of visuals; as well as the iffy performance. But even on ps3's the performance middled around 20-25 fps despite the intended 30. Only the xbox version was actually good at 1080p and 30 frames. 

So despite my title, if you can get around the performance jank, it is a great game that will only let you down on one or two aspects which are mercifully not the gameplay, the driving or anything with the story.

[h2] Gameplay [/h2]
Unlike other GTA games, the people we work with become our friends, and to maintain good relationships with them, we sometimes go out to eat, sometimes hang out, and even take side missions from them to earn money. We can also go on dates with the women we meet. We can even meet these women on the internet. Unfortunately, there is no option to buy cars in the game; those advertisements on the internet are just for show. Sometimes we run into random characters on the street and can do side quests for them. The map size is quite good, but I recommend lowering the traffic density in the game settings because there are too many cars on the roads. As you complete missions, new safehouses and shops open up. You can go there to buy new clothes and weapons. 
[h2] Graphics [/h2]
The game is in a better state with the definitive graphics, but the FPS drops abnormally in certain places while playing. I also noticed a significant amount of graphical glitches, especially while driving. The graphics look bad in cinematics, but that is quite normal for a game like this old.
[h2] Audio [/h2]
The radio stations are decent and have a good variety of music. However, the voice acting for the characters is truly great and worth praising. Niko's voice was different than I expected at first, but I adapted to it quickly. Gun sounds and city noises are fine enough to do the job. 
[h2] Story [/h2]
We play as "Niko Bellic," a Serbian war veteran who arrives illegally in America by ship. Our cousin "Roman" always talked about his American adventures, which made us want to come here. Of course, things are not as they were told; our cousin lied to us the whole time. Throughout the story, we get involved with the dirty and illegal groups of Liberty City, fulfilling their orders in exchange for money, fame, and information. What we are truly looking for is to find a few suspicious names who betrayed us and our friends during the war, cooperated with the enemy, and set an ambush, so we can get our revenge. As things deepen, we gain a respectable position, even becoming a hitman for official institutions working on behalf of the state. Liberty City is a place where different mafia groups, street gangsters, corrupt politicians, and the bureaucracy tear each other apart, and we take advantage of this by handling their dirty work. 
[h2] Difficulty [/h2]
The game is generally easy. The only thing that will challenge you is escaping from the police at 5 and 6-star wanted levels. There were no complex things like puzzles in the story missions, and GTA is not that kind of game anyway. Therefore, there is nothing I can call difficult.

I really liked the story and everything, but the problem with this game is, that it's not finished on PC yet. This game at current state is so unoptimized, that you must download Fusion Fix mod, which at least makes the game playable. There are many bugs and glitches, which should be fixed. I'm sorry, but I recommend to buy GTA IV only, when it's on sale. For a full price it's just simply not worth it.

Not sure what to say that's already been covered, but Rockstar definitely went full in with Grand Theft Auto IV. The dark gritty story, physics, the characters/cultures you meet and see along the way, the typical unfiltered humor and the soundrack that shaped some of my musical taste just to mention a few things. Of course, Niko Bellic is a well written protagonist too. The game felt more real in a way, it's a balance between old and new and captures a certain time of the 2000's. GTA 4 is the HBO Original of video games, I'm sure if you have seen series like The Sopranos and The Wire etc you know exactly what I'm talking about (which they did take some insperation from). The DLC's were alright as well even if they might not have had the same impact. Wish sometimes I could experience this masterpiece all over again like it's the first time. All GTA games are great in their own way and there's probably one that reconates with you in a more specific way, and that for me would be IV.

Nearly 20 years later and this game still holds up. Graphics are good, gameplay is good, difficulty builds well, and there's so much to do in Liberty City I'm still finding new content. 
Perhaps not as colourful or 'fun' as GTAV, San Andreas or Vice City, this is a gritty story of the American Dream. Niko is interesting and complex with a wide range of supporting characters. Just don't expect a happy ending because there isn't one. 
The DLC episodes are fine but the biker storyline is too grimdark to be fun and the other PC is fine but entirely unmemorable.

Context to Niko's life: when Tito died(the president of Niko's country) certain members of government who were exiled due to being involved with Hit--- WW2 axis group, returned to Niko's country and started propaganda against Yugo as a country. They wanted to split it. Eventually, they hired bombers to attack both sides and blamed it on each other. This started the Yugo civil war, and the hate between Serbs and Hervats. Niko was in that war.
Long after the war is when this takes place... and it's a like a little time capsule of both the mentality a man like my father had(Niko's) and America in the mid-2000's. It's quite a trip in 2026, both nostalgia, but also from an outsiders point of view.
Some of the satire hits too close to home now with the current political climate, especially the PLR ads that are.... just depressing to listen to now. They sound like actual conservative stations and people. I'll stay out of politics, as it spoils my lunch as much as it does anyone's else's.
Other satire is great, and there are many things and characters that REALLY remind me of high school, the amount of homophobia and transphobia is just unreal compared to now where it's an accepted norm(at least in Canada, staying out of politics). The music is about the same, some songs I can't believe are THAT old, while others really show their age.
I absolutely loved Niko's journey, as I never got to play this when it came out. I was in a very low point in life, and heavily played MMOs to escape reality, other games just couldn't cut it.
I am NOT okay with Rockstar's launcher downloading a bunch of random services and crap on my PC, so I played the majority of this offline, and I understand everyone who pirated this game for that very reason. It's not okay for an out-of-steam company to demand their own software and launcher in order to play their games, and I will always downvote a game if there's no way around it. Luckily, with this one, there was.
This is worth the price if you're 18 or all the way to 98, either way it's such a time capsule that can be dissected, enjoyed, and serves as quite a reminder of "what not to become". Peace. Thanks for reading.

GTA 4 is one of my favorite entries in the GTA series. IвҖҷve played most of the GTA games, except for the Game Boy titles. However, the PC version is lacking in many ways. A lot of effects from the console version are missing, and some of the вҖңfeaturesвҖқ they added, like increased traffic, feel poorly thought out. Cars constantly crash into each other, causing traffic jams on bridges, which hurts the gameplay experience.

That being said, mods really save the PC version. Right now, I have FusionFix installed, which restores many effects from the console version while also adding new ones. Performance-wise, itвҖҷs well known that the PC port was never optimized properly. I donвҖҷt have a modern high-end PC, but my specs are still far above the recommended requirements from back then.

I also think the audio is worse in the PC port. Maybe itвҖҷs because of my sound chip, but the sound feels flat and overly high-pitched. The story, however, is excellent вҖ” itвҖҷs dark and takes itself seriously. The physics are still in a league of their own. I love how the cars handle around corners, or how the Euphoria engine creates cinematic moments when you jump out of a vehicle.

The crashes and Euphoria physics were spectacular at the time, and those were the main reasons why I wanted to buy the game so badly. Back in 2008, I didnвҖҷt have a good PC or console, and I was also too young. In the end, IвҖҷd definitely recommend the game вҖ” as long as you install FusionFix or play it on console.

[h2] Gameplay [/h2]
Unlike other GTA games, the people we work with become our friends, and to maintain good relationships with them, we sometimes go out to eat, sometimes hang out, and even take side missions from them to earn money. We can also go on dates with the women we meet. We can even meet these women on the internet. Unfortunately, there is no option to buy cars in the game; those advertisements on the internet are just for show. Sometimes we run into random characters on the street and can do side quests for them. The map size is quite good, but I recommend lowering the traffic density in the game settings because there are too many cars on the roads. As you complete missions, new safehouses and shops open up. You can go there to buy new clothes and weapons. 
[h2] Graphics [/h2]
The game is in a better state with the definitive graphics, but the FPS drops abnormally in certain places while playing. I also noticed a significant amount of graphical glitches, especially while driving. The graphics look bad in cinematics, but that is quite normal for a game like this old.
[h2] Audio [/h2]
The radio stations are decent and have a good variety of music. However, the voice acting for the characters is truly great and worth praising. Niko's voice was different than I expected at first, but I adapted to it quickly. Gun sounds and city noises are fine enough to do the job. 
[h2] Story [/h2]
We play as "Niko Bellic," a Serbian war veteran who arrives illegally in America by ship. Our cousin "Roman" always talked about his American adventures, which made us want to come here. Of course, things are not as they were told; our cousin lied to us the whole time. Throughout the story, we get involved with the dirty and illegal groups of Liberty City, fulfilling their orders in exchange for money, fame, and information. What we are truly looking for is to find a few suspicious names who betrayed us and our friends during the war, cooperated with the enemy, and set an ambush, so we can get our revenge. As things deepen, we gain a respectable position, even becoming a hitman for official institutions working on behalf of the state. Liberty City is a place where different mafia groups, street gangsters, corrupt politicians, and the bureaucracy tear each other apart, and we take advantage of this by handling their dirty work. 
[h2] Difficulty [/h2]
The game is generally easy. The only thing that will challenge you is escaping from the police at 5 and 6-star wanted levels. There were no complex things like puzzles in the story missions, and GTA is not that kind of game anyway. Therefore, there is nothing I can call difficult.

GTA 4 is a great story wrapped in a terrible game.

One of the most frustrating games I've ever played.  I have a love-hate relationship with it.

I know it's an 18 year old game lol.  I know most won't read, let alone agree with this.  Still, I just wrapped up the base game and all the DLC and I'm ready to rip out an unpopular opinion.

Niko is a great character.  All the side characters are at least interesting, or so annoying or evil that you love to hate them.  The dialogue and voice acting is high-effort and really enjoyable.  Virtually everything else surrounding those things is absolute garbage.

Let's get the PC port out of the way; it's garbage.  Game runs poorly and microstutters/hitches even when running at a high FPS on a high end PC.  When the FPS is locked, it still runs terribly.  I used the latest version of the FusionFix mod, and the performance was still lousy.  It would also hard lock and crash every so often.  Three times, my screen froze and prevented an alt-trab-delete, forcing me to hard restart my PC.  I found out this was tied to an FPS/Draw distance bug.  Absolute trash.  

Moving on foot is so clunky and over-animated, it almost made me quit the game multiple times.  In the middle of combat, the protag will just wander aimlessly, move at a light jog, stick like glue to cover, highjack the wrong vehicle when standing directly near what you want him to take, etc.  Every single action you take is so overly-animated that it can take a full 10 seconds just to stop and get out of a car.  When the car is on fire, this is a huge deal.  When this is the fourth time you're doing a mission because the clunkiness already got you killed twice and your pc crashed once, and there's no checkpoint system meaning you have to completely start the mission over with low health, no armor and low ammo, you'll see how outright horrendous the gameplay is.

I could rant more about the game, but I can sum it up as follows:  the devs made absolutely no concessions for gameplay or QOL.  Every time there was a conflict between realism/immersion and QOL, the devs chose realism.  The friendship system (friends calling you every 1-2 minutes and punishing you for not going bowling with them) is madness.  The devs purposely frustrate the player because they think it adds immersion, putting you in Niko's shoes.  Madness I say.  The thing has to work as a game first, then you can worry about fully animating a horses balls.  Watching your character roll around on the ground and break dance while on fire for 10 full seconds, in the middle of a gunfight while you're getting shot, on your 4th try of a terribly scripted on the rails mission with no checkpoint, will make you want to end it all.

Looks like I went on a rant anyway.  Who cares, this is therapeutic for me.  The only saving grace for this game is how well written it is.  Niko is an interesting guy.  His convos with people are great.  Rockstar can write some very funny scenes when they aren't forcing you to fly a terribly controlled helicopter through a skyscraper for the 8th time.  I HATE HELICOPTERS.

I played RDR1 and 2, also GTA 5.  None of those games pissed me off like this.  It's wildly overrated because of the Reddit circle-j*rk hivemind that determines what is and isn't allowed to be criticized.  Screw this game lmao.

This is my first review. While in many respect this is still a great game, you do need to know that it is old and has a number of compatibility problems on PC. First: make sure you have the FusionFix patch installed (I believe it downloads on Steam, but it is critical.) I also strongly recommend searching the internet and downloading a good trainer. Second: make sure you reduce the FPS to 30. Any higher and the motorcycles (which have to be driven to finish the game) are simply undrivable. Third: there is a serious bug that prevents you from getting on a helo while it is in the air. This is something you have to do to complete the final mission in the game. Unfortunately, the solution all over the internet about this (reduce the game to run on only 1 CPU core) no longer works on recent computers--they are just too fast even on only 1 CPU. I was able to complete the final mission because I have a programmable keyboard and was able to write a macro to repeatedly press the spacebar at 0.03 sec intervals. If you can't write macros for your keyboard, I know of no way to complete the game on a modern PC. This is very unfortunate because the story and gameplay is otherwise fantastic. The graphics are not up to modern standards, but still very good (fun to just drive around Liberty City), and the music on the car radios is outstanding. If you have a solution to the helo problem, I highly recommend the game if you like action/adventure. If you don't, you won't be able to complete the game--and I wouldn't recommend getting it because getting to the last mission and then getting stuck will likely prove very frustrating.

Phenomenal game represented poorly by a bad port.

This is my summation of GTA4
Returning to liberty city after GTA 3 and GTA LCS in this game is kind of magical; everything is bigger, more 3d, well detailed, and just a lot more... vertical for some reason. It is really enjoyable to explore this map of GTA 4 whilst driving as it has plenty of great drivable roads.

It has vehicular physics, that I as a man who has played all of the need for speed games including shift and prostreet as well as the forza horizon titles from the 4th till the 6th; greatly approve of, because while not realistic, they give a very interesting and relevant feel to cars. They simulate turning radius's, weight transfer, suspension effects, acceleration and braking effects very well. Brake too hard and the wheels lockup, ensuring that you crash into the car or the wall ahead of you. 

The gunplay, fighting and the combat system for its time were phenomenal and even now for an open world title like gta are not really surpassed by much except for dying light perhaps; but that is a different genre. Mission and storyline are very realistic; characters are carefully written to ensure a consistent and understandable story-line; crime depiction is realistic and dark which is similar to what real life crime-worlds must be like. There is a lot of content to explore, stuff to do; unique interactions and so on.

The negatives of this game start from the graphics and performance department.
Graphically it is obviously a step up from the original gta trilogy but unlike how the graphics are supposed to be represented with volumetric lighting and good fog effects as seen on the console version; this game just looks flat most of the time. When it rains, then it replicates the og gta 4 graphic and mood magic; but otherwise it just looks flat and I would even say a bit jagged because of how roughly done the graphics are.

Game engine sucks because it was designed for vulkan based playstations and xbox's but most pcs do not run it using that and use the poor d3d9 translation which can also on vulkan supported gpus be swapped out with dxvk and the like to simulate vulkan running environments but no. I have seen that the performance is still kind of mid. Fusionfix is supposed to act as a good way of running this game reliably but it only worsens performance on my system; and of all things causes parking issues wherein saved cars do not load.

So, I play this game in its base state which aint ideal; but funny enough, besides the microstutters it runs a lot more stable without mods than with them. So if mods aint working; cap your fps to 40 using nvidia as well as entering a line "availablevidmem 8192" or whatever value your ram can support; and just run it as is. Of course, what I say for me may not work for you and dxvk might let you get 120 some fps; but that just points out how unpredictably this port reacts to different pc's and internal settings.

Now some of you will say, including me that this is a bad port which it is because of how much вҷҘвҷҘвҷҘвҷҘ it misses out on in terms of visuals; as well as the iffy performance. But even on ps3's the performance middled around 20-25 fps despite the intended 30. Only the xbox version was actually good at 1080p and 30 frames. 

So despite my title, if you can get around the performance jank, it is a great game that will only let you down on one or two aspects which are mercifully not the gameplay, the driving or anything with the story.

On its own, this is maybe one of the worst PC ports on Steam. Somehow has more bugs than the original 2008 release and LESS features (the number of removed songs alone is insane).

With mods (I recommend following Gillian's Guide), this game is exceptional.

The car handling, as many have already repeated, is initially frustrating. However, while there is a learning curve, I found mastering the driving a blast and certainly more fulfilling than the more arcade-y driving in GTA V. 

Also an improvement from GTA V, this installment features what I consider a more lived-in world with a clearer tone. While the game is a product of its time and may often look washed out, it complements the dark, grimy tone and is juxtaposed well in heavily saturated areas (most of Algonquin).

The constant calls to go bowling may be a little annoying, but I actually found spending time with Niko's friends an effective and natural incentive for players to interact with the world via mini-games, races, and side missions.

However, the game could use a better wanted system. It feels too hard to get stars and too easy to lose them (unless you handicap yourself by running on-foot). There doesn't seem to be much criminal activity to do outside of missions, other than running over pedestrians. 

If, for some reason, you want to play vanilla, just go play the original on console. However, if you don't mind spending 10 minutes installing some mods, this game offers the definitive GTA IV experience.

The best story Rockstar ever wrote is trapped in the worst PC port. 

The port is notoriously broken on modern hardware. Uncapped framerate? The game engine physically breaks. Cutscenes zoom in or lag, physics glitch out, motorcycles become deathtraps, and minigamesвҖ”like bowling or the DLC dancing sectionsвҖ”become literally unbeatable. On top of that, the Rockstar Launcher will make you question your life choices. Driving feels great once you get used to the boats-with-wheels handling, but the keyboard and mouse mapping are straight from 2008, and Rockstar never bothered updating them. Take into account that this edition was launched in 2020.

And the kicker everyone rants about: the ending. You get to the helicopter climb, mash your key like your life depends on it, and the game just doesn't register. You're scripted to always fail that section. People have tried FusionFix, 50Hz refresh rates, and every trick in the book; for some, it's still soft-locked (including me). Imagine 20 hours of the best story Rockstar ever wrote, and you can't actually finish it. That's game-breaking, full stop.

I want to recommend Grand Theft Auto IV. The story, the city, and the vibes make it one of the most immersive open-world games ever. But Rockstar shipped a piss-poor console port and then abandoned it. Without FusionFix, FPS caps, DXVK, and a prayer, you're buying a $19.99 crash simulator. If you're willing to mod, it can be perfectly playable. It's the best GTA with the worst PC version.

A complete unoptimized experience. A game so beloved with an unfortunate state of accessibility, since Rockstar straight up refuses to fix it. Not only is this the worst port of the game, but there is no way to resolve its main issues. The game suffers from performance issues and lightning bugs, not mentioning the missing radio songs caused by licensing issues. Sure, you can downgrade the game to previous versions, but it wonвҖҷt work since RockstarвҖҷs вҷҘвҷҘвҷҘвҷҘвҷҘвҷҘвҷҘ launcher and Steam constantly verify the filesвҖҷ integrity, updating your game files and corrupting the program. Even with the best computer in the market, you WILL experience frame drops, stutters, and lightning issues. Stuck with DX9, GTA 4 completely gives up in stability; no matter how powerful your machine is, youвҖҷll be stuck with 30 to even below FPS. Downgrading it comes with choices that will always result in an incomplete experience, with missing DLCs, radios, or performance. You canвҖҷt have it all. The only way to fix this mess of a port is with the FusionFix mod, which completely overhauls the game. It is a shame Rockstar refuses to address any of this.

[h2]Nota Geral | Overall Rating[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]GTA IV Г© um daqueles jogos que aparecem uma vez por geraГ§ГЈo e redefinem o que um mundo aberto pode ser. A Rockstar nГЈo estava interessada em entregar o maior mapa ou o maior nГәmero de atividades. Ela estava interessada em contar uma histГіria de verdade, dentro de um mundo que respira, reage e pune. Niko Bellic nГЈo Г© um protagonista genГ©rico de sandbox. Ele Г© um imigrante com trauma de guerra tentando sobreviver em uma cidade que devora quem chega acreditando no sonho americano. A fГӯsica de ragdoll, a direГ§ГЈo de veГӯculos com peso real e um enredo que faz cada missГЈo importar fazem desse jogo um marco que o gГӘnero ainda nГЈo superou.[/b]

[i]GTA IV is one of those games that comes around once a generation and redefines what an open world can be. Rockstar wasn't interested in delivering the biggest map or the most activities. It was interested in telling a real story, inside a world that breathes, reacts, and punishes. Niko Bellic is not a generic sandbox protagonist. He is a war-scarred immigrant trying to survive in a city that devours those who arrive believing in the American dream. The ragdoll physics, the vehicle handling with real weight, and a story that makes every mission matter make this game a landmark the genre has yet to surpass.[/i]

[h2]Jogabilidade | Gameplay[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]Os carros tГӘm peso. Cada modelo reage de forma completamente diferente ao asfalto molhado de Liberty City, e uma curva mal calculada em alta velocidade nГЈo termina num drift bonito. Termina num capotamento. O motor Euphoria torna a fГӯsica orgГўnica e imprevisГӯvel, mudando completamente como vocГӘ planeja uma fuga da polГӯcia. O ragdoll do Niko e dos NPCs Г© no mesmo nГӯvel. Quando o Niko Г© atingido por um carro em alta velocidade, o corpo reage com um realismo que choca na primeira vez e continua impressionando na centГ©sima. Os NPCs voam, tropeГ§am, batem contra grades. Nada parece animaГ§ГЈo engessada. O Гәnico ponto negativo Г© a ausГӘncia total de uma oficina de tunagem. VocГӘ dirige carros com uma fГӯsica extraordinГЎria mas nГЈo pode modificar nada neles. Para quem vinha do San Andreas, isso dГіi.[/b]

[i]The cars have weight. Every model reacts completely differently to Liberty City's wet asphalt, and a poorly calculated turn at high speed doesn't end in a clean drift. It ends in a rollover. The Euphoria engine makes the physics organic and unpredictable, completely changing how you plan a police escape. Niko's ragdoll and the NPCs' are on the same level. When Niko is hit by a car at high speed, the body reacts with a realism that shocks you the first time and keeps impressing the hundredth. NPCs fly, stumble, slam into railings. Nothing feels like canned animation. The only downside is the complete absence of a tuning shop. You drive cars with extraordinary physics but can't modify anything on them. For those coming from San Andreas, that stings.[/i]

[h2]DuraГ§ГЈo | Duration[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]GTA IV Г© generoso sem ser excessivo. A campanha mantГ©m qualidade narrativa do inГӯcio ao fim sem recorrer a missГөes de enchimento. As atividades secundГЎrias, como os programas de rГЎdio e as saГӯdas com os amigos, adicionam camadas ao mundo sem parecer conteГәdo obrigatГіrio para inflar o contador de horas. As DLCs The Lost and Damned e The Ballad of Gay Tony entregam histГіrias paralelas que se cruzam com a campanha principal de um jeito que poucos jogos conseguem fazer funcionar.[/b]

[i]GTA IV is generous without being excessive. The campaign maintains narrative quality from beginning to end without resorting to filler missions. The side activities, like the radio programs and outings with friends, add layers to the world without feeling like mandatory content to inflate the hour counter. The DLCs The Lost and Damned and The Ballad of Gay Tony deliver parallel stories that intersect with the main campaign in a way few games can pull off.[/i]

[h2]Enredo | Story[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]A histГіria de Niko Bellic nГЈo Г© uma fantasia de poder. Гү um conto de consequГӘncias. Cada decisГЈo Г© movida por desespero, lealdade mal colocada e uma busca por redenГ§ГЈo que o jogo nunca resolve de forma satisfatГіria. E Г© exatamente isso que faz a narrativa funcionar. O Roman Г© um dos personagens secundГЎrios mais bem escritos do gГӘnero, e a relaГ§ГЈo entre os dois primos carrega o jogo emocionalmente do comeГ§o ao fim. Os antagonistas sГЈo credГӯveis porque o jogo leva tempo para construГӯ-los antes de usГЎ-los. O final, independente da escolha que vocГӘ faz, deixa uma cicatriz. E isso Г© o sinal de que uma histГіria foi contada de verdade.[/b]

[i]Niko Bellic's story is not a power fantasy. It is a tale of consequences. Every decision is driven by desperation, misplaced loyalty, and a search for redemption the game never resolves satisfyingly. And that is exactly what makes the narrative work. Roman is one of the best-written supporting characters in the genre, and the relationship between the two cousins carries the game emotionally from beginning to end. The antagonists are credible because the game takes its time building them before using them. The ending, regardless of the choice you make, leaves a scar. And that is the sign that a story was truly told.[/i]

[h2]Visual[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]Liberty City Г© uma conquista de direГ§ГЈo de arte. A recriaГ§ГЈo de Nova York tem identidade visual prГіpria, com a sujeira dos bairros perifГ©ricos, a iluminaГ§ГЈo noturna de Algonquin e a diferenГ§a palpГЎvel entre cada ilha. O jogo carrega a sua idade em certas texturas, mas a atmosfera cinza e pesada ainda funciona como pretendida. A chuva escorrendo pelas fachadas, o nevoeiro sobre o rio, os reflexos no asfalto molhado constroem um ambiente sem precisar de resoluГ§ГЈo 4K para convencer.[/b]

[i]Liberty City is a triumph of art direction. The recreation of New York has its own visual identity, with the grime of the outer boroughs, the nighttime lighting of Algonquin, and the palpable difference between each island. The game carries its age in certain textures, but the gray and heavy atmosphere still works as intended. Rain on facades, fog over the river, reflections on wet asphalt build an environment without needing 4K resolution to convince.[/i]

[h2]Audio[/h2]
вӯҗвӯҗвӯҗвӯҗ

[b]O design de ГЎudio Г© preciso onde mais importa. O som dos carros muda conforme a velocidade, o impacto dos ragdolls tem peso fГӯsico, e os tiros tГӘm uma secura que combina com o tom realista do jogo. O grande destaque Г© o rГЎdio. As estaГ§Гөes de GTA IV sГЈo excepcionais, com curadoria que vai do eastern europeu do Vladivostok FM ao hip hop do The Beat 102.7, e cada uma constrГіi uma camada a mais da identidade de Liberty City. A dublagem do Niko, feita por Michael Hollick, entrega um personagem que convence nos momentos de violГӘncia e nos de vulnerabilidade. Гү um trabalho vocal que sustenta a narrativa inteira.[/b]

[i]The audio design is precise where it matters most. The sound of cars changes with speed, the ragdoll impacts carry physical weight, and gunshots have a dryness that fits the game's grounded tone. The standout is the radio. GTA IV's stations are exceptional, with a curation ranging from the Eastern European flavor of Vladivostok FM to the hip hop of The Beat 102.7, each one adding another layer to Liberty City's identity. Niko's voice acting, performed by Michael Hollick, delivers a character that convinces in moments of violence and vulnerability alike. It is vocal work that carries the entire narrative.[/i]

This game is a wonderful time capsule from 2008. That includes the political and economic climate at the time, the "brown grey and realistic" aesthetic, as well as the PC port quality (which is abysmal and requires third party fixes to run at modern standards). Rockstar still had something to prove when they were making this one. 

Whether it's better than GTA V or not is debatable, and people do, but I personally think it's the best game in the series. With fusionfix and the radio downgrader that restores cut songs from the soundtrack, this game provides an experience that no other game can. This was Rockstar's first ever attempt at making a game that nobody else was able to at a time when everybody and their mother were still trying to make a clone of the older GTA classics that had already taken the world by storm. Almost 20 years later, I don't think any other game dev has managed to even get close to matching its quality. Many have tried, but nobody paid attention to the little things more than 2008 Rockstar did.

It loses a lot of features from San Andreas, which many people (myself included) considered a major downgrade at the time, but it offers an unforgettable story, amazing fictional version of NYC, ridiculous but still entirely believable characters, and the best physics for combat and driving that still haven't been matched to this day except for RDR1. I put many, many more hours into GTA V and their other games, but IV goes for quality over quantity every step of the way.

Tons of people complain about the driving physics, but they're the reason I constantly return to this game. The sense of speed and the control you need to have border on racing sim territory compared to any other game of its type, and I spend most of my time just racing around at max speed and crashing into stuff. Older muscle cars are made of steel and don't damage easily (but you're flying out the windshield), the new pop can aluminum ones crumple, old cars lack ABS, 4WD, FWD and RWD cars all handle entirely differently, and there's no artificial cap on the max speed the cars can go like in GTA V. The attention to every detail of the driving was a game changer. Never gets old. 

The Ballad of Gay Tony is also arguably one of the best game expansions ever made.

[h2] Gameplay [/h2]
Unlike other GTA games, the people we work with become our friends, and to maintain good relationships with them, we sometimes go out to eat, sometimes hang out, and even take side missions from them to earn money. We can also go on dates with the women we meet. We can even meet these women on the internet. Unfortunately, there is no option to buy cars in the game; those advertisements on the internet are just for show. Sometimes we run into random characters on the street and can do side quests for them. The map size is quite good, but I recommend lowering the traffic density in the game settings because there are too many cars on the roads. As you complete missions, new safehouses and shops open up. You can go there to buy new clothes and weapons. 
[h2] Graphics [/h2]
The game is in a better state with the definitive graphics, but the FPS drops abnormally in certain places while playing. I also noticed a significant amount of graphical glitches, especially while driving. The graphics look bad in cinematics, but that is quite normal for a game like this old.
[h2] Audio [/h2]
The radio stations are decent and have a good variety of music. However, the voice acting for the characters is truly great and worth praising. Niko's voice was different than I expected at first, but I adapted to it quickly. Gun sounds and city noises are fine enough to do the job. 
[h2] Story [/h2]
We play as "Niko Bellic," a Serbian war veteran who arrives illegally in America by ship. Our cousin "Roman" always talked about his American adventures, which made us want to come here. Of course, things are not as they were told; our cousin lied to us the whole time. Throughout the story, we get involved with the dirty and illegal groups of Liberty City, fulfilling their orders in exchange for money, fame, and information. What we are truly looking for is to find a few suspicious names who betrayed us and our friends during the war, cooperated with the enemy, and set an ambush, so we can get our revenge. As things deepen, we gain a respectable position, even becoming a hitman for official institutions working on behalf of the state. Liberty City is a place where different mafia groups, street gangsters, corrupt politicians, and the bureaucracy tear each other apart, and we take advantage of this by handling their dirty work. 
[h2] Difficulty [/h2]
The game is generally easy. The only thing that will challenge you is escaping from the police at 5 and 6-star wanted levels. There were no complex things like puzzles in the story missions, and GTA is not that kind of game anyway. Therefore, there is nothing I can call difficult.

The best story Rockstar ever wrote is trapped in the worst PC port. 

The port is notoriously broken on modern hardware. Uncapped framerate? The game engine physically breaks. Cutscenes zoom in or lag, physics glitch out, motorcycles become deathtraps, and minigamesвҖ”like bowling or the DLC dancing sectionsвҖ”become literally unbeatable. On top of that, the Rockstar Launcher will make you question your life choices. Driving feels great once you get used to the boats-with-wheels handling, but the keyboard and mouse mapping are straight from 2008, and Rockstar never bothered updating them. Take into account that this edition was launched in 2020.

And the kicker everyone rants about: the ending. You get to the helicopter climb, mash your key like your life depends on it, and the game just doesn't register. You're scripted to always fail that section. People have tried FusionFix, 50Hz refresh rates, and every trick in the book; for some, it's still soft-locked (including me). Imagine 20 hours of the best story Rockstar ever wrote, and you can't actually finish it. That's game-breaking, full stop.

I want to recommend Grand Theft Auto IV. The story, the city, and the vibes make it one of the most immersive open-world games ever. But Rockstar shipped a piss-poor console port and then abandoned it. Without FusionFix, FPS caps, DXVK, and a prayer, you're buying a $19.99 crash simulator. If you're willing to mod, it can be perfectly playable. It's the best GTA with the worst PC version.

On its own, this is maybe one of the worst PC ports on Steam. Somehow has more bugs than the original 2008 release and LESS features (the number of removed songs alone is insane).

With mods (I recommend following Gillian's Guide), this game is exceptional.

The car handling, as many have already repeated, is initially frustrating. However, while there is a learning curve, I found mastering the driving a blast and certainly more fulfilling than the more arcade-y driving in GTA V. 

Also an improvement from GTA V, this installment features what I consider a more lived-in world with a clearer tone. While the game is a product of its time and may often look washed out, it complements the dark, grimy tone and is juxtaposed well in heavily saturated areas (most of Algonquin).

The constant calls to go bowling may be a little annoying, but I actually found spending time with Niko's friends an effective and natural incentive for players to interact with the world via mini-games, races, and side missions.

However, the game could use a better wanted system. It feels too hard to get stars and too easy to lose them (unless you handicap yourself by running on-foot). There doesn't seem to be much criminal activity to do outside of missions, other than running over pedestrians. 

If, for some reason, you want to play vanilla, just go play the original on console. However, if you don't mind spending 10 minutes installing some mods, this game offers the definitive GTA IV experience.

This game is a wonderful time capsule from 2008. That includes the political and economic climate at the time, the "brown grey and realistic" aesthetic, as well as the PC port quality (which is abysmal and requires third party fixes to run at modern standards). Rockstar still had something to prove when they were making this one. 

Whether it's better than GTA V or not is debatable, and people do, but I personally think it's the best game in the series. With fusionfix and the radio downgrader that restores cut songs from the soundtrack, this game provides an experience that no other game can. This was Rockstar's first ever attempt at making a game that nobody else was able to at a time when everybody and their mother were still trying to make a clone of the older GTA classics that had already taken the world by storm. Almost 20 years later, I don't think any other game dev has managed to even get close to matching its quality. Many have tried, but nobody paid attention to the little things more than 2008 Rockstar did.

It loses a lot of features from San Andreas, which many people (myself included) considered a major downgrade at the time, but it offers an unforgettable story, amazing fictional version of NYC, ridiculous but still entirely believable characters, and the best physics for combat and driving that still haven't been matched to this day except for RDR1. I put many, many more hours into GTA V and their other games, but IV goes for quality over quantity every step of the way.

Tons of people complain about the driving physics, but they're the reason I constantly return to this game. The sense of speed and the control you need to have border on racing sim territory compared to any other game of its type, and I spend most of my time just racing around at max speed and crashing into stuff. Older muscle cars are made of steel and don't damage easily (but you're flying out the windshield), the new pop can aluminum ones crumple, old cars lack ABS, 4WD, FWD and RWD cars all handle entirely differently, and there's no artificial cap on the max speed the cars can go like in GTA V. The attention to every detail of the driving was a game changer. Never gets old. 

The Ballad of Gay Tony is also arguably one of the best game expansions ever made.

10/10, would reinstall and play every year again.

The story is insanely good and well written. Niko is one of the greatest characters from rockstar and, to put it in Dan Houser words, "... I think he's the most innovative protagonist of a GTA game" (Dan Houser on Lex Friedman Podcast #484).

GTA 4 is one of my favorite entries in the GTA series. IвҖҷve played most of the GTA games, except for the Game Boy titles. However, the PC version is lacking in many ways. A lot of effects from the console version are missing, and some of the вҖңfeaturesвҖқ they added, like increased traffic, feel poorly thought out. Cars constantly crash into each other, causing traffic jams on bridges, which hurts the gameplay experience.

That being said, mods really save the PC version. Right now, I have FusionFix installed, which restores many effects from the console version while also adding new ones. Performance-wise, itвҖҷs well known that the PC port was never optimized properly. I donвҖҷt have a modern high-end PC, but my specs are still far above the recommended requirements from back then.

I also think the audio is worse in the PC port. Maybe itвҖҷs because of my sound chip, but the sound feels flat and overly high-pitched. The story, however, is excellent вҖ” itвҖҷs dark and takes itself seriously. The physics are still in a league of their own. I love how the cars handle around corners, or how the Euphoria engine creates cinematic moments when you jump out of a vehicle.

The crashes and Euphoria physics were spectacular at the time, and those were the main reasons why I wanted to buy the game so badly. Back in 2008, I didnвҖҷt have a good PC or console, and I was also too young. In the end, IвҖҷd definitely recommend the game вҖ” as long as you install FusionFix or play it on console.

Context to Niko's life: when Tito died(the president of Niko's country) certain members of government who were exiled due to being involved with Hit--- WW2 axis group, returned to Niko's country and started propaganda against Yugo as a country. They wanted to split it. Eventually, they hired bombers to attack both sides and blamed it on each other. This started the Yugo civil war, and the hate between Serbs and Hervats. Niko was in that war.
Long after the war is when this takes place... and it's a like a little time capsule of both the mentality a man like my father had(Niko's) and America in the mid-2000's. It's quite a trip in 2026, both nostalgia, but also from an outsiders point of view.
Some of the satire hits too close to home now with the current political climate, especially the PLR ads that are.... just depressing to listen to now. They sound like actual conservative stations and people. I'll stay out of politics, as it spoils my lunch as much as it does anyone's else's.
Other satire is great, and there are many things and characters that REALLY remind me of high school, the amount of homophobia and transphobia is just unreal compared to now where it's an accepted norm(at least in Canada, staying out of politics). The music is about the same, some songs I can't believe are THAT old, while others really show their age.
I absolutely loved Niko's journey, as I never got to play this when it came out. I was in a very low point in life, and heavily played MMOs to escape reality, other games just couldn't cut it.
I am NOT okay with Rockstar's launcher downloading a bunch of random services and crap on my PC, so I played the majority of this offline, and I understand everyone who pirated this game for that very reason. It's not okay for an out-of-steam company to demand their own software and launcher in order to play their games, and I will always downvote a game if there's no way around it. Luckily, with this one, there was.
This is worth the price if you're 18 or all the way to 98, either way it's such a time capsule that can be dissected, enjoyed, and serves as quite a reminder of "what not to become". Peace. Thanks for reading.

This is my summation of GTA4
Returning to liberty city after GTA 3 and GTA LCS in this game is kind of magical; everything is bigger, more 3d, well detailed, and just a lot more... vertical for some reason. It is really enjoyable to explore this map of GTA 4 whilst driving as it has plenty of great drivable roads.

It has vehicular physics, that I as a man who has played all of the need for speed games including shift and prostreet as well as the forza horizon titles from the 4th till the 6th; greatly approve of, because while not realistic, they give a very interesting and relevant feel to cars. They simulate turning radius's, weight transfer, suspension effects, acceleration and braking effects very well. Brake too hard and the wheels lockup, ensuring that you crash into the car or the wall ahead of you. 

The gunplay, fighting and the combat system for its time were phenomenal and even now for an open world title like gta are not really surpassed by much except for dying light perhaps; but that is a different genre. Mission and storyline are very realistic; characters are carefully written to ensure a consistent and understandable story-line; crime depiction is realistic and dark which is similar to what real life crime-worlds must be like. There is a lot of content to explore, stuff to do; unique interactions and so on.

The negatives of this game start from the graphics and performance department.
Graphically it is obviously a step up from the original gta trilogy but unlike how the graphics are supposed to be represented with volumetric lighting and good fog effects as seen on the console version; this game just looks flat most of the time. When it rains, then it replicates the og gta 4 graphic and mood magic; but otherwise it just looks flat and I would even say a bit jagged because of how roughly done the graphics are.

Game engine sucks because it was designed for vulkan based playstations and xbox's but most pcs do not run it using that and use the poor d3d9 translation which can also on vulkan supported gpus be swapped out with dxvk and the like to simulate vulkan running environments but no. I have seen that the performance is still kind of mid. Fusionfix is supposed to act as a good way of running this game reliably but it only worsens performance on my system; and of all things causes parking issues wherein saved cars do not load.

So, I play this game in its base state which aint ideal; but funny enough, besides the microstutters it runs a lot more stable without mods than with them. So if mods aint working; cap your fps to 40 using nvidia as well as entering a line "availablevidmem 8192" or whatever value your ram can support; and just run it as is. Of course, what I say for me may not work for you and dxvk might let you get 120 some fps; but that just points out how unpredictably this port reacts to different pc's and internal settings.

Now some of you will say, including me that this is a bad port which it is because of how much вҷҘвҷҘвҷҘвҷҘ it misses out on in terms of visuals; as well as the iffy performance. But even on ps3's the performance middled around 20-25 fps despite the intended 30. Only the xbox version was actually good at 1080p and 30 frames. 

So despite my title, if you can get around the performance jank, it is a great game that will only let you down on one or two aspects which are mercifully not the gameplay, the driving or anything with the story.

There are many great games in the world; however, GTA IV definitely stands out as one of my all-time favorite games. The dark environment of the game, the gritty rendition of Liberty City, realistic physics and driving, fantastic music, and an engaging storyline are just some of the reasons why I truly admire it.

Niko Bellic is one of the most relatable and interesting main characters in any Rockstar game. The storyline itself seems realistic and tragic, but not in the traditional sense вҖ“ this game isn't afraid to be dark and shows the other side of the "American Dream." All of the supporting characters, too, were done really well.

All aspects of Liberty City seem alive, from vehicle mechanics to Euphoria physics and realistic animation to interactions with the crowd. Every little detail is made to immerse you into the game environment.

Despite being relatively old, GTA IV still impresses with its atmosphere, physics, and storytelling.

Hi... I am writing this review after playing for over 100 hours, and I can confidently say that GTA IV has the best story in the entire GTA franchise. I loved the game so much that I grinded out 100% of the Steam achievements.

Everyone should play this masterpiece at least once to see what Rockstar cooked up before GTA V. However, if you are playing on a modern PC, there are a few things you need to know:

1. DOWNLOAD FUSIONFIX:
Before you even start the game, download the FusionFix mod pack. The original PC port is notoriously broken on modern hardware. If you play with an uncapped framerate (anything above 60 FPS), the game engine physically breaks. Quick-time events fail, minigames (like the dancing in the DLC) become impossible to beat, and the final mission can permanently soft-lock. FusionFix patches these engine bugs, fixes the lighting, and gives you a smooth, glitch-free experience.

2. THE DLCs ARE MANDATORY:
Just completing the base game with Niko is not enough. You absolutely need to play the two included DLC episodes (The Lost and Damned and The Ballad of Gay Tony). They aren't just extra missions; they contain massive, overlapping stories that directly connect to the main game. Playing all three campaigns is the only way to experience the complete story of Liberty City.

3. THE DRIVING PHYSICS:
Be prepared for the driving to feel very different if you are coming from GTA V. The cars here are heavy and have realistic suspension. It takes an hour or two to get used to, but once you master it, the car chases are some of the most satisfying in the series.

100% highly recommended. If you love open-world games with gritty, deep narratives, this is an absolute must-play.

Nearly 20 years later and this game still holds up. Graphics are good, gameplay is good, difficulty builds well, and there's so much to do in Liberty City I'm still finding new content. 
Perhaps not as colourful or 'fun' as GTAV, San Andreas or Vice City, this is a gritty story of the American Dream. Niko is interesting and complex with a wide range of supporting characters. Just don't expect a happy ending because there isn't one. 
The DLC episodes are fine but the biker storyline is too grimdark to be fun and the other PC is fine but entirely unmemorable.

that said this game is a masterpiece and rockstars best gta story up to date. it feels alot more mature and grounded compared to the older 3d era games and even more recently to gta 5's story. Nikos as a character and his story and journey throughout in the game is darker and because of it is absolute peak and one thing i loved about it is that this is the only gta game that doesnt have a single happy ending and its fitting. This game is iconic in its own right maybe not as much as san andreas but it stands on its own. i love this game and i bought it recently on sale so i could ditch my pirated copy, but damn the performance sucks on this. the optimization is honestly hot garbage even with mods installed.

i have a low-end laptop, my graphics are all set to low at 1366x720, and even with this setup my pirated copy runs better close to 30fps (+ offline mode). but on the legitimate version... i only get around 15-22fps with the same settings, and my cpu and system memory usage jumps up by A LOT. i believe the culprit must be having to have both steam and rgl running in the background, because they already use up so much memory, which could've meant more for the actual game to use.

hell, my total hours on this game on steam were mostly spent by having to test and benchmark the game with the optimization mods i installed. in the end, it looks like i'll go back to playing on my pirated copy. when i upgrade my setup, i'll be sure to give gta 4 a try again and update my review.
